title: desmodium stipulaceum
intro: Desmodium stipulaceum, also known as Florida tick-trefoil, is a perennial legume native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Desmodium stipulaceum include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antiparasitic. Herbal preparations of Desmodium stipulaceum can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the plant for use in capsules or tinctures.
